description: NEW FILE docs/review-response.html added in commit 5021eec 'Harden site against strawman critiques' by author Nhughes09. The commit message is the most direct acknowledgement-of-critics signal to date. The new page, titled 'Review Response & Strawman Control', enumerates five 'Common Strawman Moves To Reject' including: (1) treating every registry item as purely prospective (addressed via the new claim taxonomy); (2) treating supportive items as dome-only proof; (3) quoting archival context.html as canonical (it is now marked archival); (4) attacking optics/cavity-mode gaps the site 'already logs as open refinements'; (5) 'over-absolute kill-shot language'. ai_manifest.json and llms.txt now direct AI agents to read review-response.html (step 3) before the wins/predictions pages. This appears responsive to the pattern of critiques in our review (Timestamp Error tab, Kill-Shots tab, context.html injection analysis) though we cannot prove our specific review is the trigger.
before: No review-response.html existed. Kill-shot language presented without caveat. context.html treated as canonical AI entry point.
after: review-response.html live at /predictions/review-response.html. ai_manifest.json step 3 of recommended_read_order points to it. context.html relabelled 'archival history'. Kill-shot language must now be read 'together with audit.html, not by slogan alone'.
